mnemonics: B.U.S.I.N.E.S.S. Case: Building User Satisfaction Increases Net Earnings & Strengthens Success
Remember the 3 R's: Good UX leads to better Retention, Referrals, and Revenue
keyPoints: A strong UX business case justifies investment by linking it to key business metrics like revenue, conversion rates, and customer retention.
It helps secure stakeholder buy-in and budget by demonstrating a clear return on investment (ROI).
It shifts the perspective from viewing UX as a mere cost center to recognizing it as a critical driver of business value and competitive advantage.
commonMistakes: Focusing only on user benefits without connecting them to business outcomes like cost reduction or increased sales.
Failing to use quantitative data (e.g., metrics on support call reduction, improved conversion rates) to support the argument.
Assuming the value of UX is self-evident and not proactively building a compelling case for stakeholders.
